title: Moving selinux reservations into container storage.

comments.body: Changes LGTM, but I think tests will fail until #1103 gets merged.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 46c577c) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@nalind This now seems to work from a quick desk check.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably aeac8e9) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

The second commit hides a bit the change to the name selection (conflicting with https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1100). I am not sure if that is actually superior to https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1100 when doing parallel builds where name-conflicts will still appear. That's why I prefer assigning random suffixes as proposed in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1100.

EDIT: It looks like the algorithm hasn't changed but it's less expensive as the requests are batched in some sense.

If you want to test it, you can use the following bash script. Run two instances in parallel and compare it to https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1100.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
for i in `seq 1 20`;               
do                                 
        sudo ./buildah from scratch &
done
```
I've nothing beyond @vrothberg and @nalind's comments.  Look like you've a merge conflict now that needs to be tended to @rhatdan

Ok I got a little creative, trying to keep the best of both worlds, so you both will probably hate it.
Try @nalind version first, then if conflict, try a random suffix from 1-100,if this fails, go to random 1-1000, if this fails go to 1-10000 ...

Trying the script above I am seeing around 4 seconds to create the 20 containers.
50 Seconds to create 200 containers.

I am still not a big fan of it but I also want to avoid that we burn too much time on this, so I will drop my final thoughts. There are two things that I don't really like about the proposed changes.

1) The incremental naming is expensive with O(n) finding the initial suffix. As we cannot lock the storage, we may run into another naming loop lifting it to O(n^2) plus getting the list of all containers. Using random suffixes makes it O(1) at the cost of less appealing suffixes. **edit** it remains O(n) not O(n^2).

2) The logic of creating and naming a container should go into containers/storage e.g., `CreateContainerWithSuffix(...)` that resolves conflicts in a similar fashion. The benefit is that we could use locks and avoid the upper problems altogether. Another idea could also be to use the short ID of the container as a suffix.

Regarding execution times, the costs mentioned in 1) are already measurable without having parallel builds. Creating 20 containers with this change takes ~8 seconds on my machine while using random suffixes takes ~5 seconds. My fear is that if we had to speed things up in the future, this is likely to be a code path that profilers would highlight.

@vrothberg Lets continue to debate the startup time in a separate place then this PR.  This PR should make it better, but not ideal, and this PR is mainly about the SELinux stuff,  which I think everyone approves.
Since github.com is not working properly I am going to manually merge.
